response.justification: The candidate has strong software development fundamentals and 16+ years of industry experience, but faces critical disqualifying gaps. Most significantly, native-level Japanese language proficiency is a stated requirement and completely absent from the resume. The candidate's recent career trajectory (last 7 years) has been primarily web/full-stack development in TypeScript and Node.js, moving away from the C-based embedded systems and security technologies this role demands. While they have some foundational embedded and networking experience from 2014-2018, there is no evidence of recent work in security features, network switch development, or Linux kernel modification—all core aspects of the position.
strengths: 16+ years of professional software development experience demonstrating ability to master multiple programming languages and technologies
Proven systems-level thinking through infrastructure work, hardware control, and CI/CD setup at companies like Altom and National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, open-source contributions, and mentoring through workshops and courses
concerns: No mention of native Japanese language proficiency, which is a stated job requirement
Recent career trajectory (7+ years) focuses on web/full-stack development rather than embedded systems or C programming for security features
No evidence of experience with required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), networking switch development, or Linux kernel modification

key_factors.factor: Recent web-focused career trajectory vs. embedded systems domain
